prompt:
A Helios Cultural Content & Operations Management System that includes a public-facing library and an internal operations console for managing a curated corpus of classical poetry and related reference content in a fully offline environment. Regular Users use a Vue.js web interface to search and browse poems, authors, and annotated excerpts with instant feedback such as keyword highlighting, “did you mean” suggestions, and smart filters that can be combined (keyword plus author plus dynasty plus genre/tag plus line snippet plus meter/tune pattern). The search experience supports simplified/traditional character conversion on input and display, plus synonym expansion that can be toggled per query to keep results predictable for scholars. Users can access core browsing and previously viewed search results while offline, with clear network-state indicators, a “queued actions” drawer, and resumable downloads of local content packs so a library kiosk or field researcher can keep working through brief outages. Staff roles include Administrator, Content Editor, Reviewer/Arbitrator, Marketing Manager, and Crawler Operator. Marketing Managers create offline-only pricing rules for premium annotations and downloadable collections, including coupons (for example, $10 off $50, 15% off, or single-use redeem codes), limited-time campaigns (MM/DD/YYYY hh:mm AM/PM windows), basic flash-sale/group-buy constructs, and member tiers; the UI must clearly show the effective price and explain stacking (at most one coupon plus one campaign discount, never exceeding 40% total discount, and excluding already-member-priced items). After a user consumes a premium item or receives support, they can submit a review with 1–5 star ratings across accuracy, readability, and value plus free text, and can file a complaint that routes to arbitration with visible status and outcomes.

The backend uses Gin to provide REST-style endpoints consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend over the local network or localhost, enforcing role-based access control and session timeouts (30 minutes idle) with username/password only; passwords are salted and hashed, and sensitive notes in complaints are encrypted at rest with an application-managed key stored locally. MySQL persists content, pricing rules, orders/entitlements, reviews, complaints, and operational metrics, while full audit logs capture who changed what and when, including before/after diffs and an optional approval workflow toggle that, when enabled, requires an Administrator sign-off within 48 hours for deletions or bulk edits. Data editing supports create/update/delete for structured reference entities with topology/geometry validation for any stored spatial features (reject self-intersections; enforce maximum vertex count of 10,000 per shape), plus bulk edits with rollback to any prior revision within 30 days. Full-text search is implemented with locally built inverted indexes refreshed on a schedule or on publish, using ranking that weights exact phrase matches above synonyms and boosts matches in titles and first lines; suggestions are derived from local query history and dictionary files. Distributed crawling runs entirely on-prem across multiple nodes with elastic scheduling, per-host rate limits (default 1 request/second) and job quotas (for example, 10,000 pages/day), checkpointed resume-from-breakpoint, retry with exponential backoff capped at 5 attempts, and status/duration metrics for operators. Offline assurance includes a client-side cache for critical datasets, a persistent retry queue for writes (reviews, complaints, edits) with idempotency to prevent duplicates, plus local performance monitoring and crash reports stored on disk for Administrator review without any third-party services.
